data.name: Claude
data.tagline: Anthropic's AI assistant for analysis, writing, and coding
data.description: Claude is Anthropic's flagship AI assistant, known for nuanced reasoning, long-context document analysis, and safe, honest responses. Claude.ai offers a free tier and a Pro subscription with access to the most capable Claude models, file uploads, and Projects for persistent memory across conversations.
data.highlight: Best-in-class long-context reasoning with a strong safety and honesty focus.

data.name: ChatGPT
data.tagline: OpenAI's AI assistant used by over 200 million people worldwide
data.description: ChatGPT is the world's most widely used AI assistant, powered by GPT-5.5 Instant (default as of May 2026) with GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Thinking available on paid plans. It supports text, image, voice, and file inputs, and offers custom GPTs, a code interpreter, web browsing, and image generation — all in one interface.
data.highlight: The most widely used AI assistant with the broadest feature set of any consumer product.

data.name: Gemini
data.tagline: Google's AI assistant with real-time Search and up to 2M-token context
data.description: Gemini is Google's consumer AI assistant powered by the Gemini 3.x model family. It integrates tightly with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Gmail), supports file and image inputs, and can ground answers in live Google Search results. Gemini Advanced unlocks Gemini 3.1 Pro with a 2M-token context window — the largest of any frontier model.
data.priceNote: Free tier · $19.99/mo Advanced (Google One AI Premium)
data.highlight: Deep Google Search integration and 1M-token context window in Advanced tier.

data.name: Microsoft Copilot
data.tagline: Microsoft's AI assistant built on GPT-5.5 with live Bing Search
data.description: Microsoft Copilot is powered by OpenAI's GPT-5.5 models and Bing's real-time web index, delivering up-to-date answers with citations. It's integrated across Windows 11, Edge, Microsoft 365, and Teams, making it the default AI layer across Microsoft's product ecosystem for hundreds of millions of users.
data.highlight: Native across Windows and Microsoft 365 with live Bing-grounded answers.

data.name: Grok
data.tagline: xAI's assistant with real-time X/Twitter data and Aurora image generation
data.description: Grok is xAI's AI assistant built into the X platform (formerly Twitter), with unique real-time access to the X data firehose. Grok 4.1 is available to all users; Grok 4.3 Beta (Apr 2026) adds multimodal video understanding and enhanced reasoning. The standalone Grok app offers image generation via Aurora. Grok tends to have fewer content restrictions than other major assistants.
data.priceNote: Free on X (limited) · $8/mo X Premium for full Grok access
data.highlight: Unique real-time awareness via live access to the X/Twitter data firehose.

data.name: Meta AI
data.tagline: Meta's free AI assistant built into WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook
data.description: Meta AI is powered by Llama 4 and embedded directly into WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, and Messenger, reaching over 3 billion monthly active users. The standalone Meta AI web app supports image generation with Imagine and real-time web search. It's one of the few major AI assistants available entirely free with no subscription required.
data.highlight: Completely free assistant embedded across Meta's 3B+ user social platforms.

data.name: Le Chat
data.tagline: Mistral AI's fast, privacy-friendly European assistant
data.description: Le Chat is Mistral AI's consumer and business chat interface, running on Mistral's frontier models including Mistral Large 2 and the Pixtral multimodal model. Built with European data privacy standards in mind, it supports web search grounding and offers a generous free tier, making it popular among privacy-conscious users and European businesses.
data.highlight: Fast, GDPR-compliant European assistant with strong multilingual support.

data.name: Jasper
data.tagline: AI writing assistant built for marketing and brand teams
data.description: Jasper is an AI writing platform purpose-built for marketing teams, offering brand voice training, campaign workflows, and a library of templates for ads, blog posts, emails, and social media. The Brand Voice feature learns your company's tone and applies it consistently across all generated content.
data.highlight: Brand Voice training ensures every piece of content sounds consistently on-brand.

data.name: Copy.ai
data.tagline: AI-powered GTM platform for sales and marketing copy at scale
data.description: Copy.ai started as a copywriting tool and has evolved into a go-to-market (GTM) AI platform that automates sales outreach, content workflows, and marketing operations at scale. It offers 90+ copywriting templates, a workflow builder for multi-step automation, and integrations with CRMs and marketing platforms.
data.highlight: Evolved beyond copywriting into a full GTM workflow automation platform.

data.name: Grammarly
data.tagline: AI writing assistant for grammar, clarity, and tone
data.description: Grammarly is the leading AI writing assistant for real-time grammar checking, style suggestions, and tone adjustments. The GrammarlyGO generative AI layer adds text generation, rewriting, and ideation directly into browsers, Google Docs, Microsoft Word, and email clients. Trusted by over 30 million daily users.
data.highlight: 30M+ daily users rely on its real-time grammar and clarity suggestions.

data.name: Notion AI
data.tagline: AI writing and Q&A built directly into your Notion workspace
data.description: Notion AI adds generative AI capabilities directly into Notion's workspace, enabling users to draft, edit, summarize, and ask questions about their pages and databases. The AI Q&A feature searches across an entire connected workspace to surface answers from meeting notes, docs, and wikis instantly.
data.priceNote: Free Notion plan · $10/mo AI add-on per member
data.highlight: Q&A across your entire workspace surfaces answers from any page instantly.

data.name: Writesonic
data.tagline: AI content platform for SEO articles, ads, and landing pages
data.description: Writesonic is an AI content platform combining a writing assistant with an article writer optimized for SEO. Chatsonic adds real-time web search to conversational AI. The platform covers blog posts, ads, product descriptions, and landing pages with built-in Surfer SEO integration for search ranking optimization.
data.highlight: Built-in Surfer SEO integration optimizes articles for search rankings.

data.name: QuillBot
data.tagline: AI paraphrasing, summarizing, and grammar checking tool
data.description: QuillBot specializes in AI-powered paraphrasing with multiple writing modes (Standard, Fluency, Formal, Creative, Academic), a document summarizer, grammar checker, and citation generator. Widely used by students, researchers, and content creators for rewriting and condensing existing text.
data.highlight: Multiple paraphrase modes give fine-grained control over tone and formality.

data.name: Wordtune
data.tagline: AI writing companion that rewrites, summarizes, and enriches text
data.description: Wordtune is an AI writing companion that offers real-time rewrite suggestions for improving clarity, tone, and conciseness. Unlike grammar checkers, it rewrites full sentences to be more compelling. The Spices feature generates factual enhancements, analogies, and examples to enrich any draft.
data.highlight: Spices feature adds factual enhancements and analogies to enrich any draft.

data.name: Cursor
data.tagline: AI-first code editor built on VS Code with deep codebase context
data.description: Cursor is a fork of VS Code that puts AI at the center of the coding experience. Its Composer feature allows multi-file edits from a single prompt, while the Chat panel answers questions about the entire codebase using embeddings. Cursor integrates models from Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google and has become the fastest-growing AI coding IDE.
data.highlight: Composer enables AI-driven multi-file edits across an entire codebase at once.

data.name: GitHub Copilot
data.tagline: AI coding assistant integrated across GitHub and major IDEs
data.description: GitHub Copilot is the most widely deployed AI coding assistant, integrated into VS Code, Visual Studio, JetBrains IDEs, and GitHub's web editor. It provides inline completions, a chat sidebar, pull request summaries, and Copilot Workspace for autonomous multi-step coding tasks. Backed by OpenAI GPT-5.5 and Claude Sonnet 4.6.
data.priceNote: Free for verified students/OSS maintainers · $10/mo Individual · $19/mo Business
data.highlight: The most widely deployed AI coding assistant with deep IDE-native integration.

data.name: Windsurf
data.tagline: Agentic AI coding IDE with Cascade for autonomous task completion
data.description: Windsurf (by Codeium) is an AI-first IDE featuring Cascade, an agentic coding system that autonomously plans and executes multi-step programming tasks. Unlike traditional tab-completion tools, Cascade reads terminal output, runs commands, and iterates until tasks are complete — one of the most autonomous coding environments available.
data.highlight: Cascade's agentic loop reads terminal output and iterates until the task is done.

data.name: Replit
data.tagline: Browser-based IDE with AI agents that build and deploy full-stack apps
data.description: Replit is a cloud-based coding environment where you can write, run, and deploy code directly from the browser. Replit Agent takes a plain-English prompt and autonomously scaffolds a full-stack application, installs dependencies, configures databases, and deploys to a live URL — no local setup required.
data.highlight: Build and deploy full-stack apps from a plain-English prompt, entirely in the browser.

data.name: Tabnine
data.tagline: Privacy-first AI code completion that runs locally or in the cloud
data.description: Tabnine is an AI code completion tool that prioritizes developer privacy, offering fully on-device model options alongside cloud models. It integrates with all major IDEs and supports 80+ programming languages. The enterprise tier enables training a personalized model on your company's private codebase.
data.highlight: On-device model option keeps your code entirely private without cloud exposure.

data.name: Codeium
data.tagline: Free AI code completion and chat for 70+ programming languages
data.description: Codeium offers free AI code completion and an in-IDE chat assistant supporting over 70 programming languages across all major editors. It's one of the few AI coding tools with a fully featured free plan for individual developers, plus enterprise features including custom model fine-tuning on private codebases.
data.highlight: Fully featured free plan for individuals with 70+ language support.

data.name: Midjourney
data.tagline: The most popular AI image generator for artistic and photorealistic visuals
data.description: Midjourney is the leading AI image generation service, renowned for stunning artistic and photorealistic imagery. It operates through a Discord bot and a web interface at midjourney.com. Midjourney v6.1 and the v7 model deliver exceptional detail, photorealism, and style control via text prompts and reference images.
data.highlight: Sets the benchmark for artistic quality in AI image generation.

data.name: DALL-E 3
data.tagline: OpenAI's image generator with best-in-class prompt adherence
data.description: DALL-E 3 is OpenAI's flagship image generation model, available via ChatGPT and the OpenAI API. It excels at precise prompt following, accurately rendering text within images, and generating coherent scenes with multiple subjects. Integration into ChatGPT makes it one of the most accessible AI image generators.
data.priceNote: Via ChatGPT Free (limited) · Plus $20/mo · API from $0.04/image
data.highlight: Best prompt adherence of any major image model, including accurate in-image text.

data.name: Stable Diffusion
data.tagline: Open-source image model powering thousands of self-hosted tools
data.description: Stable Diffusion is the foundational open-source image generation model from Stability AI. Because its weights are publicly available, it powers an enormous ecosystem of fine-tuned models, LoRAs, and tools like ComfyUI, Automatic1111, and InvokeAI. SDXL and SD3 are the latest major versions with improved quality.
data.highlight: Open weights power a massive ecosystem of custom fine-tuned community models.

data.name: Adobe Firefly
data.tagline: Adobe's commercially safe AI image generator trained on licensed content
data.description: Adobe Firefly is Adobe's family of generative AI models trained exclusively on licensed and public domain content, making outputs commercially safe to use without rights concerns. Deeply integrated into Photoshop (Generative Fill), Illustrator, and Express, it also generates vector graphics, patterns, and 3D textures.
data.priceNote: Free 25 credits/mo · Adobe Creative Cloud plans from $9.99/mo
data.highlight: Trained exclusively on licensed content — outputs are commercially safe to use.

data.name: Ideogram
data.tagline: AI image generator with best-in-class text rendering inside images
data.description: Ideogram is an AI image generation platform that specializes in accurately rendering readable text within images — historically the hardest challenge for diffusion models. It supports photorealistic and stylized outputs, poster design, logo drafts, and typography-heavy compositions. Ideogram 2.0 added Canvas for iterative editing.
data.highlight: Industry-leading accuracy for rendering legible text inside generated images.

data.name: FLUX
data.tagline: Black Forest Labs' state-of-the-art image model with open weights
data.description: FLUX is a family of image generation models from Black Forest Labs, founded by the original creators of Stable Diffusion. FLUX.1 [dev] and [schnell] are open-weight models available for self-hosting, while FLUX.1 [pro] is offered via API. FLUX delivers exceptional photorealism, prompt adherence, and human anatomy accuracy.
data.highlight: Outperforms earlier diffusion models on human anatomy realism and prompt adherence.

data.name: Canva AI
data.tagline: AI design and image generation built into the world's most popular design tool
data.description: Canva has integrated AI throughout its platform, including Magic Media (image and video generation), Magic Edit (AI inpainting and editing), Magic Design (auto-layout from a prompt), and Dream Lab (powered by FLUX). With 170+ million users, it's the most accessible entry point to AI image creation for non-designers.
data.highlight: AI generation embedded in a familiar design tool used by 170M+ people.

data.name: Runway
data.tagline: Leading AI video generation platform trusted by professional filmmakers
data.description: Runway is the leading professional AI video generation company, with its Gen-3 Alpha model producing high-quality, consistent video clips from text and image prompts. Used by major film studios and creative agencies for visual effects and generative video workflows. It supports text-to-video, image-to-video, and motion brush controls.
data.highlight: Gen-3 Alpha is trusted by film studios for professional-grade AI video generation.

data.name: Sora
data.tagline: OpenAI's text-to-video model generating cinematic clips up to 60 seconds
data.description: Sora is OpenAI's video generation model capable of producing realistic, cinematic video clips up to 60 seconds from text or image prompts. It understands physics, complex multi-subject scenes, and camera movement instructions. Available to ChatGPT Plus and Pro subscribers via the Sora.com interface.
data.highlight: Generates coherent 60-second clips with realistic physics and camera movement.

data.name: Kling AI
data.tagline: Kuaishou's high-quality video generation with up to 2-minute clips
data.description: Kling AI is a video generation platform by Kuaishou that produces high-quality video clips up to 2 minutes from text or image prompts. It features a lip-sync mode, camera controls, and a motion brush for targeted animation. Kling has gained global popularity for consistent, high-realism video at competitive pricing.
data.highlight: Generates up to 2-minute video clips with camera controls and lip-sync support.

data.name: Pika
data.tagline: AI video creation and editing with scene modification features
data.description: Pika is an AI video generation and editing platform for generating short video clips from text or images, and modifying existing videos by changing scenes, adding effects, or altering characters. Pika 2.0 introduced Pikadditions (place objects into video) and Pikaffects (apply visual effects) for creative control.
data.highlight: Pikadditions lets you place any object into an existing video scene seamlessly.

data.name: HeyGen
data.tagline: AI avatar video creation for marketing, training, and localization
data.description: HeyGen is an AI video platform specializing in realistic digital avatars that speak from a text script. Widely used for product demos, marketing videos, and corporate training. Its video translation feature localizes videos into 40+ languages while automatically syncing lip movements to the translated audio track.
data.highlight: Translate videos into 40+ languages with AI lip-sync matched to the new audio.

data.name: Dream Machine
data.tagline: Luma AI's fast, high-quality text-to-video and image-to-video generator
data.description: Luma Dream Machine is a text-to-video and image-to-video generator from Luma AI, known for speed and high-quality smooth motion. It generates 24fps video clips with realistic physics and consistent camera movement. Luma's 3D scene capture background (NeRF technology) gives it strong spatial understanding.
data.highlight: Fast video generation with smooth, physically realistic camera movement.

data.name: ElevenLabs
data.tagline: The leading AI voice platform for text-to-speech and voice cloning
data.description: ElevenLabs is the leading AI voice synthesis company, offering hyper-realistic text-to-speech, instant voice cloning from a 1-minute audio sample, and a Voice Library with thousands of community voices. It supports 29 languages with natural intonation and powers voice for thousands of apps, games, and media projects.
data.highlight: Clone any voice from a 1-minute audio sample with natural 29-language support.

data.name: Suno
data.tagline: AI music generator that creates complete songs from a text prompt
data.description: Suno is an AI music generation platform that produces complete songs — vocals, instruments, and lyrics — from a short text description. Users specify genre, mood, instruments, and lyrical themes. Suno v4 delivers professional-quality audio with natural vocal performances, making it one of the most capable AI music tools available.
data.highlight: Generates complete songs with vocals, instruments, and lyrics from a single prompt.

data.name: Udio
data.tagline: AI music creation with granular style and production control
data.description: Udio is an AI music generation platform that competes with Suno, offering text-to-music generation with strong control over genre, style, BPM, key, and instrumentation. Praised for high production quality and musical coherence, it also supports remixing and extending generated tracks for longer compositions.
data.highlight: Fine-grained BPM, key, and style controls for precise AI music composition.

data.name: OpenAI Whisper
data.tagline: Open-source speech recognition model with near-human accuracy across 99 languages
data.description: Whisper is OpenAI's open-source automatic speech recognition model, trained on 680,000 hours of multilingual audio. It transcribes and translates speech across 99 languages with near-human accuracy, even in noisy environments. Available for free local inference or via the OpenAI API at $0.006 per minute.
data.highlight: Open-source ASR with near-human accuracy across 99 languages — free to self-host.

data.name: Murf AI
data.tagline: AI voiceover studio with 200+ voices for creators and businesses
data.description: Murf AI is an AI voiceover platform offering 200+ natural-sounding voices across 20+ languages, designed for professional voiceovers for videos, podcasts, e-learning, and ads. The studio interface provides pitch, speed, and emphasis controls, and lets users sync voice audio to video timelines without recording equipment.
data.highlight: 200+ studio-quality voices with video timeline sync for professional voiceovers.

data.name: Descript
data.tagline: AI podcast and video editor that lets you edit media by editing text
data.description: Descript is an AI-powered audio and video editor built around a novel concept: it transcribes your recording and lets you edit the media by editing the transcript text. Overdub lets you clone your voice to fix mistakes by typing new words. Features also include automatic filler-word removal and Studio Sound noise reduction.
data.highlight: Edit audio and video by editing the text transcript — a uniquely intuitive workflow.

data.name: Adobe Podcast
data.tagline: AI audio enhancement that makes any microphone sound studio-quality
data.description: Adobe Podcast Enhance Speech uses AI to remove background noise and room echo from any audio recording, making a standard laptop microphone sound like a professional studio setup. The broader Adobe Podcast platform adds AI-powered recording, transcript-based editing, and remote podcast collaboration.
data.priceNote: Enhance Speech free · Adobe Podcast in Creative Cloud plans from $9.99/mo
data.highlight: One-click AI removes noise and reverb, making any mic sound studio-quality.

data.name: Perplexity AI
data.tagline: AI answer engine that searches the web and cites every source
data.description: Perplexity is an AI-powered answer engine that searches the web in real time and synthesizes answers with inline citations. Unlike traditional search engines, it provides direct answers rather than a list of links. Perplexity Pro adds frontier models (GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3.1 Pro) and Deep Research mode for comprehensive long-form reports.
data.highlight: Answers with real-time web citations, eliminating the need to click through 10 links.

data.name: NotebookLM
data.tagline: Google's AI research assistant grounded exclusively in your own documents
data.description: NotebookLM is Google's AI-powered research notebook that grounds all answers in the specific documents you upload — PDFs, Google Docs, YouTube videos, and web URLs. Because it only answers from your sources, hallucination is minimized. The Audio Overview feature generates a podcast-style conversation summarizing your materials.
data.priceNote: Free · NotebookLM Plus via Google One AI Premium ($19.99/mo)
data.highlight: Audio Overview transforms your research documents into a listenable podcast.

data.name: Consensus
data.tagline: AI search engine for peer-reviewed scientific research with consensus metrics
data.description: Consensus is an AI-powered search engine built specifically for scientific literature, indexing over 200 million research papers. It extracts and synthesizes findings from peer-reviewed studies and presents a Consensus Meter showing the level of scientific agreement across the literature for any evidence-based question.
data.priceNote: Free 20 searches/mo · $9.99/mo Premium · $9.99/mo Team per user
data.highlight: Consensus Meter shows scientific agreement levels across 200M+ indexed papers.

data.name: Elicit
data.tagline: AI research assistant for systematic literature review and data extraction
data.description: Elicit is an AI research tool designed for systematic literature reviews, automating finding, screening, and extracting data from research papers. It processes thousands of papers, extracts specific data points into structured tables, and synthesizes findings across studies. Built for researchers, scientists, and academics.
data.highlight: Automates systematic literature review by extracting structured data from thousands of papers.

data.name: Semantic Scholar
data.tagline: Free AI-powered academic search engine from the Allen Institute for AI
data.description: Semantic Scholar is a free AI-powered academic search engine from the Allen Institute for AI, indexing over 200 million scientific papers. It uses NLP to extract key findings, author influence metrics, and citation context. The AI2 Recommendation system surfaces related papers based on reading history and research interests.
data.highlight: 200M+ papers with AI-extracted key findings and intelligent citation context.

data.name: You.com
data.tagline: AI search engine with multiple research modes and source citations
data.description: You.com is an AI-powered search engine combining web search with AI chat, offering Smart mode (quick answers), Genius mode (deep multi-source synthesis), and Research mode (academic paper citations). It supports Wolfram Alpha for computational queries and allows customizable AI persona settings.
data.highlight: Multiple search modes from quick answers to deep academic research with citations.

data.name: Zapier AI
data.tagline: AI-powered workflow automation connecting 7,000+ apps
data.description: Zapier's AI features extend its automation platform with natural language Zap creation, AI actions (call any LLM mid-workflow), and AI-powered chatbots that can trigger automations. With 7,000+ app integrations, Zapier AI lets non-technical users build complex automated workflows involving AI steps without writing code.
data.highlight: Natural language Zap creation connects AI steps to 7,000+ apps without coding.

data.name: Make
data.tagline: Visual workflow automation with advanced logic and native AI modules
data.description: Make (formerly Integromat) is a visual no-code automation platform using a canvas to build complex multi-step workflows. It includes native modules for OpenAI, Anthropic, and other AI providers, and supports advanced logic like iterators, routers, and error handlers for complex automation scenarios beyond Zapier's capabilities.
data.highlight: Visual canvas with advanced logic handles complex multi-step automation scenarios.

data.name: n8n
data.tagline: Open-source workflow automation with native AI agent support and self-hosting
data.description: n8n is a source-available workflow automation tool that can be fully self-hosted for complete data control. It features a visual node-based editor and native AI agent nodes connecting to OpenAI, Anthropic, Hugging Face, and other providers. Popular in developer and privacy-conscious teams that need automation without third-party data exposure.
data.highlight: Self-hostable automation with native AI agent nodes for complete data privacy.

data.name: Otter.ai
data.tagline: AI meeting notes, transcription, and action item extraction
data.description: Otter.ai automatically records, transcribes, and summarizes meetings across Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams. Its AI Meeting Assistant joins calls automatically, captures speaker-identified transcripts in real time, and extracts action items and highlights. OtterPilot can answer questions about past meetings via a chat interface.
data.highlight: Auto-joins meetings and captures speaker-identified transcripts in real time.

data.name: Gamma
data.tagline: AI presentation builder that generates polished decks from a prompt
data.description: Gamma is an AI-powered presentation and document creation tool that generates polished, well-designed presentations, docs, and websites from a text prompt or outline. It uses flexible card-based layouts with responsive design, includes AI image generation, and supports one-click theme restyling across an entire deck.
data.highlight: Generate a polished presentation from a single prompt in under a minute.

data.name: Lindy
data.tagline: AI agents that autonomously handle email, scheduling, and business workflows
data.description: Lindy lets you create AI agents ('Lindies') that autonomously handle business workflows: triaging and replying to emails, scheduling meetings, qualifying leads, processing documents, and more. Each Lindy is a configurable agent with access to connected apps, triggered by events like incoming emails or form submissions.
data.highlight: Autonomous email-triaging and scheduling agents that act on your behalf 24/7.

data.name: Claude Code
data.tagline: Anthropic's agentic CLI for autonomous software engineering tasks
data.description: Claude Code is Anthropic's terminal-based agentic coding tool that gives Claude direct access to your filesystem, shell, and development environment. It reads and writes code across an entire repository, runs tests, debugs errors, manages git operations, and completes multi-step software engineering tasks autonomously from the command line.
data.priceNote: Usage-based via Anthropic API (Sonnet/Opus token pricing)
data.highlight: Full filesystem and shell access for autonomous multi-step software engineering.

data.name: Operator
data.tagline: OpenAI's computer-use agent that navigates the web autonomously
data.description: Operator is OpenAI's computer-use AI agent that operates a web browser autonomously to complete tasks on your behalf — booking restaurants, filling forms, shopping, and navigating websites. Built on the Computer-Using Agent (CUA) model, it handles multi-step web workflows that previously required manual human interaction.
data.highlight: Autonomously navigates websites and completes multi-step web tasks on your behalf.

data.name: Mem
data.tagline: AI-powered personal knowledge base that organizes itself automatically
data.description: Mem is an AI-native note-taking app that uses AI to automatically organize your notes, surface relevant information at the right moment, and answer questions from your personal knowledge base. Unlike traditional note apps, Mem requires no manual folder structure — its AI understands context and surfaces the right notes automatically.
data.priceNote: $14.99/mo Mem Personal · $24.99/mo Mem Teams per user
data.highlight: AI auto-organizes your notes and surfaces the right information at the right time.

data.name: Qwen 3.7 Max
data.tagline: Alibaba's flagship agentic model — 1M context, 35-hour autonomous tasks
data.description:
Qwen 3.7 Max is Alibaba's most capable model as of May 2026, built for long-horizon agentic execution — autonomously running for up to 35 hours across thousands of tool calls and code iterations without human intervention. With a 1M-token context window and 65,536 max output tokens, it ranks #1 on BenchLM's leaderboard (score 92, ahead of DeepSeek V4 Pro at 88). Available via API on Alibaba Cloud, OpenRouter, and Together AI; open weights expected around June 2026.

**Step-by-step (API):** 1) Sign up at chat.qwen.ai for free preview. 2) For API, access via OpenRouter (`qwen/qwen3.7-max`) or Alibaba Cloud DashScope. 3) Use `enable_thinking: true` for full reasoning mode or `false` for fast responses. 4) Best for: autonomous coding agents, long research pipelines, multi-step business workflow automation.
data.priceNote: API: $2.50/M input · $7.50/M output · Free preview at chat.qwen.ai
data.highlight: Autonomously executes complex tasks for up to 35 hours with 1,000+ tool calls.

data.name: L2P
data.tagline: Tencent's pixel-space image generator — no VAE artifacts, native 1K quality
data.description:
L2P (Latent-to-Pixel) from Tencent YouTu Research bypasses the VAE compression bottleneck of standard diffusion models, generating images directly in pixel space. By freezing a 6B-parameter latent diffusion backbone and training only shallow adapter layers, it transfers all learned priors into native 1024×1024 pixel generation with no latent-space color shift or blur. Weights on HuggingFace; ComfyUI nodes available for drop-in use.

**Step-by-step:** 1) `git clone https://github.com/TencentYoutuResearch/T2I-L2P`. 2) Download weights: `huggingface.co/zhen-nan/L2P`. 3) `pip install -r requirements.txt`. 4) Run the inference script with a text prompt and target resolution. 5) Alternatively, install the ComfyUI nodes (rebels-l2p or gjnave/ggf-ltp-zimage). **VRAM:** 8 GB (512px) · 24 GB recommended for native 1K.
data.priceNote: Free (open-source, self-hosted) · ~20 GB model on disk
data.highlight: Pixel-space generation eliminates the VAE color-shift and blurriness of latent models.

data.name: LiTo
data.tagline: Apple's single-photo → 3D model with view-dependent lighting and reflections
data.description:
LiTo (Surface Light Field Tokenization) is an Apple ML Research model (ICLR 2026) that reconstructs a complete 3D object with view-dependent appearance — specular reflections, Fresnel effects — from a single RGB photo. It encodes the surface light field into compact latent vectors capturing both geometry and lighting. Unlike static 3D reconstructions, the output renders correctly as the viewer moves around the object.

**Step-by-step:** 1) `git clone https://github.com/apple/ml-lito`. 2) Download `lito_new.ckpt` checkpoint from repo releases. 3) `pip install -r requirements.txt`. 4) Run the inference script with a single input image. 5) Export the 3D representation for use in game engines, AR apps, or VFX pipelines. Demos at apple.github.io/ml-lito.
data.priceNote: Free (code + checkpoint on GitHub, Apple ML Research license)
data.highlight: Extracts a view-dependent 3D model with correct reflections and lighting from one photo.

data.name: PanoWorld
data.tagline: Floor plan → navigable whole-house 3D tour with style switching
data.description:
PanoWorld (arXiv, May 2026) generates consistent, navigable multi-room 3D virtual tours from a 2D floor plan. It uses autoregressive panoramic generation with a 3D Gaussian Splatting spatial memory that prevents cross-room visual bleed, and supports text-conditioned style switching (e.g., modern → rustic). Output matches discrete-node VR tour formats used by real estate platforms.

**Status:** Research paper only — no public code or weights released yet. Best for tracking: real estate virtual tours, game level generation, interior design previsualization. Paper: arxiv.org/abs/2605.17916.
data.priceNote: Research preview — no weights released yet
data.highlight: Converts a floor plan into a navigable multi-room 3D tour with switchable design styles.

data.name: Marlin-2B
data.tagline: 2B video model — dense captions and plain-English timestamp search
data.description:
Marlin-2B is a 2-billion-parameter video VLM fine-tuned on Qwen3.5-2B, purpose-built for two tasks developers actually need: dense captioning (what happened + when, structured with second-precise timestamps) and natural-language temporal grounding (query with plain English, get exact start/end timestamps back). At ~4–5 GB, it fits a 6 GB consumer GPU, beats Qwen2.5-VL-7B by +6.4 mIoU on TimeLens-Bench, and matches Gemini-2.0-Flash on temporal grounding.

**Step-by-step:** 1) `pip install transformers`. 2) `from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoProcessor`. 3) Load: `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('NemoStation/Marlin-2B', trust_remote_code=True)`. 4) Call `.caption(video_path)` for dense timestamps or `.find(video_path, 'your query')` for NL search. **Best for:** video indexing, security/event detection, meeting search, sports highlights.
data.priceNote: Free (open-source on Hugging Face) · ~4–5 GB VRAM
data.highlight: Query any video in plain English and get back precise start/end timestamps.

data.name: Mega-ASR
data.tagline: Foundation ASR built for real-world audio where Whisper breaks down
data.description:
Mega-ASR is an open-source speech recognition model purpose-built for real-world audio — noise, far-field speech, echo, reverberation, recording artifacts, electronic distortion, and transmission dropout — covering 54 compound acoustic scenarios in a single model. Built on a Whisper backbone with LoRA routing, it achieves up to 30% WER reduction over SOTA in challenging conditions. Released May 19, 2026 with Apache 2.0 weights on Hugging Face.

**Step-by-step:** 1) `git clone https://github.com/xzf-thu/Mega-ASR`. 2) `pip install -r requirements.txt`. 3) Download weights from HuggingFace (linked in repo). 4) Run the provided inference script with your audio file. 5) Alternatively, install via Pinokio for a one-click desktop app. **Best for:** call-center audio, field recordings, podcasts with bad mics, surveillance, any pipeline where Whisper degrades.
data.priceNote: Free (Apache 2.0, self-hosted) · Under 5 GB on consumer GPU
data.highlight: Up to 30% WER reduction vs Whisper on noisy, echoing, or far-field recordings.

data.name: Stable Audio 3
data.tagline: Stability AI's open-weight music generator — full tracks up to 6 minutes
data.description:
Stable Audio 3.0 (May 20, 2026) is a family of four text-to-audio diffusion models trained entirely on licensed data. The Medium model generates music compositions up to 6 minutes 20 seconds from a text prompt. Three models (Small SFX, Small, Medium) are open-weight on Hugging Face; the Large model is API-only for music platforms.

**Step-by-step:** 1) `pip install diffusers transformers torch`. 2) Load: `stabilityai/stable-audio-3-medium` from HuggingFace (or `-optimized` for lower VRAM). 3) Pass text prompt + desired duration. 4) Output: stereo WAV file. **Model sizes:** Small SFX (~2 GB, SFX only) · Small (~3 GB, ~90s) · Medium (~6.5 GB, 6:20 min) · Large (API only). **Best for:** background music for video, game audio, podcast intros, rapid music prototyping.
data.priceNote: Small/Medium open-weight (free) · Large via Stability AI API
data.highlight: Three open-weight models generate tracks up to 6:20 min — all trained on licensed data.

data.name: LeRobot Humanoid
data.tagline: Hugging Face's open-source 3D-printed bipedal robot for AI learning research
data.description:
LeRobot Humanoid (May 21, 2026) is a fully open-source bipedal humanoid robot platform buildable for ~$2,500 from 3D-printed parts and off-the-shelf electronics. It ships as a complete stack: assembly docs, runtime control code, system identification scripts, and MuJoCo/Isaac simulation environments — enabling the full research loop of print → simulate → collect data → train → deploy. Part of the Apache 2.0 LeRobot ecosystem alongside SO-ARM100 arms and OpenArm.

**Step-by-step:** 1) `git clone https://github.com/huggingface/lerobot`. 2) Follow assembly guide at huggingface.co/blog/lerobot-humanoid. 3) 3D-print all parts from the STL files in the repo. 4) Source electronics per the BOM (motors, controllers, IMU). 5) Flash runtime and run system identification. 6) Train policies in MuJoCo sim before real-world deployment. **Best for:** robotics research, sim-to-real policy transfer, academic labs, maker community.
data.highlight: Build a real bipedal humanoid robot for $2,500 with a full open-source AI training stack.

data.name: Lovable
data.tagline: AI-powered full-stack app builder — describe it, ship it
data.description:
Lovable lets anyone build full-stack web apps by describing them in plain language. It scaffolds a React + Supabase project, writes the code, deploys it, and iterates on your feedback — no IDE required. In February 2026 it crossed $400M ARR, making it one of the fastest-growing developer tools ever. The June 2026 'Skills' feature lets teams save reusable AI workflows (design systems, accessibility checks, SEO rules) as slash commands.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Go to lovable.dev. 2) Describe your app idea in natural language. 3) Lovable generates a full React app with backend. 4) Iterate via chat. 5) Deploy with one click to a custom domain. **Best for:** founders building MVPs, marketers creating landing pages, product teams prototyping features, anyone who thinks faster than they can code.
data.priceNote: Free tier (5 messages/day) · Pro $25/mo · Teams from $49/mo
data.highlight: $400M ARR in Feb 2026 — fastest-growing developer tool. No-code to full-stack in minutes.

data.name: Bolt.new
data.tagline: In-browser AI full-stack dev environment by StackBlitz
data.description:
Bolt.new runs a full Node.js environment inside your browser and lets you build, run, edit, and deploy full-stack apps with AI assistance. It executes npm packages, handles build tools, and deploys to a public URL — all without leaving the browser tab. It supports any framework (React, Vue, SvelteKit, Astro, Next.js) and uses Claude Sonnet for code generation.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Open bolt.new. 2) Describe what you want to build. 3) Bolt scaffolds and runs the app in-browser in ~30 seconds. 4) Chat to iterate. 5) Click 'Deploy' for a shareable URL. **Best for:** rapid prototyping, hackathons, teaching, trying new frameworks without setup.
data.highlight: Runs a full Node.js runtime in the browser — build, run, and deploy without leaving your tab.

data.name: v0
data.tagline: Vercel's AI UI component generator — describe → copy React + Tailwind
data.description:
v0 by Vercel generates React UI components from natural language prompts, producing clean Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui code you paste directly into your project. It supports multi-turn iteration ('make it darker', 'add a mobile menu') and can generate complete page layouts, dashboards, and forms. v0 is deeply integrated with Next.js and deploys one-click to Vercel.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Visit v0.dev. 2) Describe the UI component ('a dark SaaS dashboard sidebar with collapsible nav'). 3) Copy the generated React/Tailwind code. 4) Paste into your project. 5) Iterate via chat. **Best for:** frontend developers bootstrapping UI, designers wanting code output, teams using shadcn/ui, Next.js projects.
data.highlight: Generates production-quality shadcn/ui + Tailwind React components from a text description.

data.name: Devin
data.tagline: Cognition's autonomous AI software engineer — plans, codes, debugs end-to-end
data.description:
Devin is Cognition's AI software engineer that autonomously handles full engineering tasks: reading requirements, planning an approach, writing code, running tests, fixing bugs, and opening pull requests — without human intervention in the loop. In June 2026, Cognition rebranded Windsurf as Devin Desktop, an agent-neutral IDE with the open Agent Client Protocol (ACP) for multi-agent orchestration. Backed at a $25B valuation after a $1B raise in May 2026.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Open devin.ai or install Devin Desktop. 2) Assign a GitHub issue or natural-language task. 3) Devin creates a plan, opens a browser/terminal, writes code, and runs tests. 4) Review the PR it opens. **Best for:** engineering teams automating routine tasks, refactoring legacy codebases, unblocking solo founders on backend work.
data.highlight: First AI software engineer raised at $25B — autonomously opens PRs and fixes bugs end-to-end.

data.name: Amazon Q Developer
data.tagline: AWS's AI coding assistant with deep AWS service knowledge
data.description:
Amazon Q Developer is AWS's AI coding and cloud assistant embedded in VS Code, JetBrains, the AWS Console, and the CLI. It suggests code completions, answers questions about your AWS architecture, explains CloudWatch logs, generates unit tests, performs security scanning, and can autonomously transform Java 8/11 to Java 17 codebases. In 2026 it handles multi-file agentic code changes and cost-optimization recommendations in the AWS Console.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Install Amazon Q Developer in VS Code (AWS Toolkit extension). 2) Sign in with an AWS Builder ID (free) or IAM Identity Center (paid). 3) Use '/dev' for agent tasks, '/explain' for code, '/fix' for bugs, '/test' for unit tests. **Best for:** AWS developers, teams doing cloud migrations, DevOps engineers, anyone using CDK or CloudFormation.
data.highlight: Deep AWS integration — explains your logs, fixes your CDK, and migrates Java codebases autonomously.

data.name: Krea AI
data.tagline: Real-time AI image generation and enhancement with live canvas editing
data.description:
Krea AI offers real-time AI generation — as you paint or sketch on the canvas, the AI generates a refined image in real time, with sub-second latency. It supports image upscaling (Enhancer), ControlNet-style guided generation, AI video generation, and a 'Flux Real-time' mode for live creative sessions. Krea is popular for concept art, product visualization, and architectural rendering.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Open krea.ai. 2) In 'Real-time' mode, sketch rough shapes. 3) Type a style prompt. 4) Watch the AI refine your sketch into a polished image in real time. 5) Use Enhancer to upscale to 4K. **Best for:** concept artists, product designers, architects, anyone who thinks visually and iterates fast.
data.highlight: Sub-second real-time generation — paint a rough sketch and watch AI refine it live.

data.name: Runway Gen-4
data.tagline: Hollywood-grade AI video generation and editing
data.description:
Runway's Gen-4 model (2026) generates cinematic video clips up to 10 seconds from text, images, or reference videos, with consistent character appearance across shots — a breakthrough called 'Reference Mode.' It supports director-style motion controls: camera movement, slow motion, style transfer, and scene compositing. Used in multiple Hollywood productions and music videos. The Act-One feature converts actor performance capture into animated characters.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Log in at runwayml.com. 2) Choose Gen-4 and upload a reference image or type a prompt. 3) Set motion style (cinematic, slow, zoom). 4) Generate. 5) Extend or edit clips in the timeline editor. **Best for:** filmmakers, music video directors, advertising agencies, game studios, solo creators building cinematic content.
data.highlight: Reference Mode keeps characters consistent across scenes — used in Hollywood productions.

data.name: Pika
data.tagline: AI video generation and editing with scene-modify and Pikaffects
data.description:
Pika 2.2 (2026) generates and edits video from text or images, with unique Pikaffect features: 'Inflate' makes flat objects 3D, 'Explode' dramatically shatters scenes, and 'Squish' flattens objects into cartoon shapes. The 'Modify Region' tool lets you selectively edit specific areas of existing video with text prompts. Popular for social media, short films, and creative experimentation.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Open pika.art. 2) Upload an image or type a text prompt. 3) Choose a Pikaffect (optional). 4) Generate. 5) Use 'Extend' to lengthen clips. **Best for:** social media creators, TikTok and Reels content, experimental filmmakers, designers wanting motion graphics.
data.highlight: Pikaffects (Inflate, Explode, Squish) create surreal, share-worthy effects no other tool offers.

data.name: Scite
data.tagline: Smart citation analysis — see how papers are cited in context
data.description:
Scite shows not just how many times a paper has been cited, but HOW it is cited — whether other papers support, contradict, or simply mention its claims. The 'Assistant' feature lets you ask research questions and get answers grounded in peer-reviewed literature with inline citations. Scite has indexed 1.8 billion citation statements from over 200 million papers.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Go to scite.ai. 2) Search for a paper or topic. 3) See the citation badges (Supporting / Contrasting / Mentioning). 4) Use Assistant: 'What does the literature say about X?' 5) Review supporting and contradicting papers side-by-side. **Best for:** academics writing literature reviews, researchers validating claims, fact-checkers, medical professionals evaluating clinical evidence.
data.highlight: 1.8B citation statements indexed — see if papers support or contradict each other, not just cite counts.

data.name: Synthesia
data.tagline: Enterprise AI video platform — professional training and comms videos at scale
data.description:
Synthesia is the enterprise standard for AI video production at scale — used by 55,000+ companies including Google, Heineken, and Reuters. Create professional training videos, product demos, and internal communications using 230+ AI avatars and 140+ languages without cameras or studios. The STUDIO feature lets L&D teams update video content by editing the script (the avatar re-renders automatically). GDPR-compliant and SOC 2 certified.

**Step-by-step:** 1) Sign up at synthesia.io. 2) Choose a template or start from scratch. 3) Select an avatar and language. 4) Paste your script. 5) Generate. 6) Share link or export MP4. **Best for:** L&D and training teams, HR onboarding, customer education, global companies needing multilingual content, regulated industries needing audit trails.
data.highlight: 55,000+ enterprise customers — update a script and the avatar video auto-regenerates.

data.name: Unitree G1
data.tagline: Commercial humanoid robot with open-source AI voice command control
data.description:
The Unitree G1 is a commercial bipedal humanoid robot with a full developer SDK and 23 degrees of freedom (43 in the EDU model). In March 2026, Unitree open-sourced UnifoLM-VLA-0, a Vision-Language-Action model enabling real-time natural-language voice control — no pre-programming required, the robot acts on spoken commands. The G1 integrates a 4-array microphone, binocular depth cameras, and 3D LiDAR.

**Step-by-step (voice control):** 1) Own a Unitree G1 ($16K base). 2) Install UnifoLM-VLA-0 from GitHub/HuggingFace (open-sourced March 2026). 3) Connect via Unitree Python SDK (support.unitree.com). 4) Speak natural-language commands — the VLA model processes audio + visual input and generates motor actions in real time. **Best for:** embodied AI research, warehouse automation, human-robot interaction, VLA model research.
data.highlight: Open-source VLA model converts spoken commands into real-time robot motor actions.
